steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The nitrogen-treated SiC fiber has a lower minimum creep rate than the two polymer-derived fibers. A lower creep rate means the material deforms under stress more slowly, indicating superior resistance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "unlike the two polymer-derived SiC fibers, the nitrogen-treated SiC fiber can substantially inhibit creep, provided that temperatures and loads are consistent.": Overstates the finding. The data show a lower creep rate for the nitrogen-treated fiber, not that only it can substantially inhibit creep. ✗
  • "aerospace composites containing the nitrogen-treated SiC fiber may have the ability to withstand mechanical stress for a longer period of time than can aerospace composites containing either of the two polymer-derived SiC fibers.": Lower creep rate directly implies the material resists deformation longer under the same stress. ✓
  • "composites based on the two polymer-derived SiC fibers have chemical properties that may improve the mechanical and thermal stability of aerospace equipment to a greater extent than do composites based on the nitrogen-treated SiC fiber.": Reverses the comparison and adds unsupported claims about chemical properties and stability. ✗
  • "the two polymer-derived SiC fibers likely hold similar potential for reducing the creep resistance of materials exposed to stress and elevated temperatures, thus prolonging the life span of aerospace machinery.": Reducing creep resistance would worsen performance, not prolong machinery life, and the passage does not support a similarity between the two polymer-derived fibers. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
